evals.prompt: Create a reusable StatCard component that shows an economic indicator name, current value, percent change, and a sparkline chart area. It should accept a custom className. Add a tooltip on the percent change showing the previous value.
evals.expected_output: A StatCard.svelte component using Card, Tooltip from shadcn-svelte, cn() for class composition, semantic color tokens including text-positive/text-negative for percent change, and lucide-svelte for any icons.
expectations: Uses cn() from $lib/utils for class merging with the className prop
Imports Card with compound pattern: import * as Card from '$lib/components/ui/card'
Imports Tooltip with compound pattern: import * as Tooltip from '$lib/components/ui/tooltip'
Uses text-positive or text-negative for the percent change color
Uses semantic color tokens (bg-card, text-foreground, etc.) not hardcoded colors
Imports icons from lucide-svelte if any icons are used
Component accepts a class prop that gets merged via cn()

evals.prompt: The delete button on dashboard cards should only show on hover for desktop but always be visible on mobile. Also show a success toast after deletion.
evals.expected_output: Modified DashboardCard.svelte using opacity transition pattern for responsive show/hide and svelte-sonner toast for feedback.
expectations: Uses opacity-based responsive pattern (opacity-0/opacity-100 with group-hover) not hidden/block
Uses md: breakpoint for the hover behavior
Parent element has the group class
Calls toast.success() or similar after deletion
Does not add a new <Toaster> component

evals.prompt: Create a settings page with a form to edit the user's display name. Put a save button in the header bar. Include a theme switcher.
evals.expected_output: A settings page using AuthenticatedLayout with navBarActions snippet for the save button, a form with Label+Input, and the existing ThemeSelector component.
expectations: Defines a navBarActions snippet for the save button in the header
Uses native <form onsubmit> pattern not on:submit
Pairs Label for= with Input id= for accessibility
Reuses existing ThemeSelector.svelte rather than building a new theme toggle
Uses Dialog or inline form pattern consistent with project conventions
